title: 국경을 넘는 신앙: 미국 기독교 주교 일본 방문 — 가정연합과 함께 하다
previewText:
국가가 한 종교 단체의 교회를 봉쇄할 때, 바다 건너편에 있는 신도들은 무엇을 말하는가.
2026년 3월 4일, 도쿄 고등재판소는 일본의 세계평화통일가정연합에 대해 해산명령 판결을 내렸다.
그로부터 몇 주 후인

blocks.text: 국가가 한 종교 단체의 교회를 봉쇄할 때, 바다 건너편에 있는 신도들은 무엇을 말하는가.
2026년 3월 4일, 도쿄 고등재판소는 일본의 세계평화통일가정연합에 대해 해산명령 판결을 내렸다.
그로부터 몇 주 후인 4월 29일, 미국에서 바다를 건너온 다섯 명의 기독교 주교와, 역시 미국에서 건너온 100명이 넘는 가정연합 2세 청년들이 도쿄의 한 행사장에 모였다.
일본의 신도들에게 이 만남은, 자신들이 고립되어 있지 않다는 사실을 세계에 보여주는 순간이 되었다.
행사장에서는 바다를 건너온 다섯 명의 주교와 일본 측 전문가, 그리고 일본과 미국의 2세 신도들이 한자리에 모여 일본에서 일어나고 있는 일에 대해 솔직하게 이야기를 나누는 토크 세션이 열렸다.
주교들의 격려의 말은 성경과 국경을 넘어선 신도들의 결속에 뿌리내리고 있었다.
패널은 천주평화연합 (UPF) 의 타마미 씨가 사회를 맡았으며, 일본 측 패널리스트로 우오타니 사무총장과 나카야마 츠키 변호사가 참석했다.
미국 측 성직자는 다음과 같다.
로널드 토마스 주교 (리컨실리에이션 어포스톨릭 미니스트리즈 / 네바다주 라스베가스)
아브람 딕슨 주교 (아 딕슨 미니스트리 / 텍사스주 휴스턴)
에드워드 바넷 주교 (그레이터 그레이스 패밀리 미니스트리 / 워싱턴 D.C.)
레너드 듀 주교 (스타 오브 호프 미셔너리 침례교회 / 캘리포니아주 샌프란시스코 베이 에어리어)
존 와츠 주교 (킹덤 라이프 처치 어포스톨릭 / 메릴랜드주 볼티모어)
우오타니 사무총장이 방문단의 성직자들에게 차례로 질문을 던졌고, 나카야마 변호사는 법적인 배경을 설명했다.
가장 먼저 발언한 로널드 토마스 주교는, 해산 소식은 충격이었지만, 기도 가운데 성경의 긴 역사에 뿌리를 둔 또 다른 시각으로 인도되었다고 말했다.
"구약과 신약 전체를 통하여, 박해가 임할 때면 그 뒤에는 반드시 약속이 따라왔습니다."
바로 그것이 자신이 도쿄에 온 이유라고 그는 말했다. 슬퍼하기 위해서가 아니라, 지금 일어나고 있는 일을 통해 자기 자신이 어떻게 성장할 수 있는지를 묻기 위해 왔다고 말하며, 그 일은 "미국을 위해서도, 일본을 위해서도 아닌, 하나님 나라를 위한 것입니다"라고 덧붙였다.
교회 폐쇄에 대해 발언한 아브람 딕슨 주교는, 가장 우려스러운 점은 판결이 내려지기 전에 교회 청산에 대한 실무적인 준비가 이미 완료되어 있었다는 점이라고 지적했다.
그는 일본 신도들을 짓누르는 현실적인 무게, 즉 다음 달의 지출을 어떻게 감당해야 할지 알 수 없는 각 교회의 상황을 인정하면서도, 그 답을 섭리적인 언어로 풀어냈다.
"하나님은 살아 계시며, 하나님께서는 그 자녀들을 위해 모든 것을 예비해 두셨습니다. 하나님은 일본을 지키고 계십니다. 여러분은 반드시 번성할 것입니다. 괜찮습니다."
에드워드 바넷 주교는, 신앙의 자유를 성경적 토대에까지 거슬러 올라가, 모세가 백성을 노예 상태에서 이끌어낸 일, 오직 하나님만을 경배하라는 계명, 그리고 예수께서 그 동일한 명령을 다시 확인하신 일을 되짚었다.
"자유는 모든 영역을 초월합니다. 국가는 육체를 구속할 수 있습니다. 영혼을 흔들 수도 있습니다. 그러나 그 어떤 인간의 힘도 영혼 그 자체에는 닿을 수 없습니다. 바로 그것이 예배의 자유라고, 저는 믿습니다."
그의 말은 일본 사례에 관한 국제적인 견해와도 맞닿아 있었다. 유엔 자유권규약위원회는 일본에 대해 "공공의 복지"를 이유로 신앙의 자유를 제한하지 말 것을 권고해 왔으며, 시민적 및 정치적 권리에 관한 국제규약 제18조는 그러한 제한을 허용하지 않는다고 지적하고 있다.
캘리포니아주 베이 에어리어에서 일본을 찾은 레너드 듀 주교는 먼저 일본 신도들의 환영에 감사를 표한 뒤, 기독교 성직자들을 종종 갈라놓는 문제로 화제를 옮겼다. 교파의 차이가 지니는 의미에 관한 이야기였다.
방문단의 주교들은 각자 다른 전통에 속해 있다고 그는 인정했다. 밖에서 보면 그렇게 갈라진 가지들은 "사교 클럽"처럼 보일 수도 있다. 그러나 그 가지들은 모두 동일한 한 몸으로 돌아간다고 그는 강조했다. "우리는 모두 하나입니다."
일본 신도들을 향한 마지막 격려의 메세지에서, 그는 그 확신을 더 깊이 풀어냈다.
"우리는 하나입니다. 예수님에 대한 믿음을 통해, 우리는 하나입니다. 그리고 우리는 같은 사명을 공유하고 있습니다. 성령과 진리 안에서, 우리는 모든 영광을 하나님께 드리며, 이곳에서 일본 사람들과 함께 하나님을 찬양하고 높임으로써, 우리는 하나인 것입니다."
법인으로서의 교회 자체가 해체되려 하는 가운데, 신도 공동체가 지니는 의미는 무엇인가. 이 물음에 대해 존 와츠 주교는 그날의 행사에서 가장 많이 인용되는 한 구절을 남겼다.
"신도의 공동체란 장소가 아닙니다. 여러분이 어디로 가든, 여러분 자신이 곧 교회입니다."
2세 신도들에게 직접 말을 건네면서, 그는 이렇게 이어 갔다.
"이 어려운 때에, 여러분의 정체성은 그리스도 그분 자신입니다. 장소가 교회가 아닙니다. 여러분 자신이 하나님께서 거하시는 교회입니다."
그는 또한 예배의 자유를, 신도들이 "부끄럼 없이 하나님 앞에 설 수 있게" 해 주는 하나님께서 주신 권리라고 말했으며, 행사장에 모인 청년들의 모습을 "하나님께서 일본에서 일하고 계시며, 하나님께서 살아 계신다"는 증거라고 짚었다.
나카야마 변호사는 행사장에서 나온 법적 질문에 답했다.
완전한 해산 이후, 일본에서 동일한 명칭으로 다시 출발하기는 매우 어려울 것이라고 그는 말했다. 그러나 종교 단체에 국가 등록을 강제하는 법률은 일본에 존재하지 않으며, 신도들에게는 집회의 권리가 남아 있다.
그는 그동안 일본 국내에서도 재판 과정에서의 부당함을 일관되게 호소해 왔다. 그러나 일본 법원만으로 판결을 뒤집는 것은 현실적으로 매우 어렵다. 그러므로 국제적인 지원이 반드시 필요하다고 강조했다.
나카아먀 변호사는 또한 재판소에서, 2022년 아베 신조 전 총리를 암살한 혐의로 유죄 판결을 받은 야마가미 테츠야의 행위에 대해 "가정연합의 해산과는 어떠한 관련도 없다"고 인정한 점을 강조했다.
가정연합 세계선교본부에 따르면, 미국 국무부는 일본이 해산의 근거로 민사 소송을 동원하고 있는 것은 자유민주주의 국가의 상식과 규범에 어긋난다고 지적한 바 있다.

에드워드 바넷 주교는 또한 가정연합의 지도자인 한학자 총재 이야기로 이어갔다.

문선명 총재의 시대로부터 이어져 온 흔들림 없는 자세를 칭송한 뒤, 그는 한 총재의 한국에서 진행 중인 재판으로 이야기를 옮겼다. 그 재판을 통해 보여 주고 있는 한 총재의 태도와 모습은, 2세 신도들에게 더 깊은 신앙과 하나님과의 더욱 큰 일치로 향하는 길을 보여 주며 그들에게 용기를 주고 있다고 그는 말했다.
"어머님(한 총재)은 흔들리지 않으십니다. 저도 흔들리지 않습니다. 이 때에, 우리는 포기하지 않습니다. 침묵하지 않습니다. 여리고의 성벽이 무너지는 그 순간까지, 우리 한 사람 한 사람이 외치고 또 외칠 것입니다."
그는 한 총재의 노벨 평화상 후보 지명을 언급하며, 일본의 신도들과 미국의 성직자들이 하나가 되어 서 있는 모습을 보고 깊이 감동했다고 말했다.
행사장의 참가자들 가운데는 "HELP" 또는 "HELP OUR CHURCH"라고 적힌 티셔츠와 손팻말을 든 모습이 곳곳에서 눈에 띄었다. 미국의 2세 신도들은 일본 가정연합에 대한 지속적인 지원 활동의 일환으로, 이러한 메세지를 티셔츠와 팻말에 담아 활동을 이어 오고 있다.
포럼의 마지막에, 와츠 주교는 이 "help"라는 단어의 라틴어 어원을, 폭풍이 오기 전 선원들이 짐을 묶을 때 사용하는 "밧줄"을 뜻하는 말로까지 거슬러 올라갔다.
"우리는 흩어지지 않을 것입니다. 하나님께서는 우리의 짐을 하나로 묶어 주고 계십니다. 많은 나라의 밧줄로, 세계의 밧줄로."
이어서 주교들은, 법적인 장애에도 불구하고 하나님의 역사는 일본의 신도들과 함께 계속 이어져 나갈 것임을 선언했다.
세계 각지의 가정연합 신도들은 일본의 신도들을 지켜보며, 이 시련을 이겨낼 수 있도록 지원을 보내고 있다.
다섯 명의 미국 기독교 주교의 이례적인 도쿄 방문은, 가정연합의 해산이 더 이상 일본 국내 종교법인의 문제가 아니라, 국경을 넘어선 신앙의 자유의 문제로 받아들여지고 있음을 보여 준다.
나카야마 변호사가 거듭 반드시 필요하다고 강조한 "국제적인 지원"은, 이날 도쿄의 행사장에서 눈에 보이는 형태로 모습을 드러냈다.
한일 2세와 전문가의 토크 세션 전체 영상: https://www.youtube.com/live/YgmIoCEpPSk?si=_2ATENpzL8ln5Dkj

title: 国境を越える信仰：米国のキリスト教司教らが訪日ー家庭連合と共に立つ
previewText:
国家が一つの宗教団体の教会を封鎖させるとき、海の向こうの信徒たちは何を語るのか。
2026年3月4日、東京高等裁判所は世界平和統一家庭連合へ解散命令の判決を下した。

blocks.text: 国家が一つの宗教団体の教会を封鎖させるとき、海の向こうの信徒たちは何を語るのか。
2026年3月4日、東京高等裁判所は世界平和統一家庭連合へ解散命令の判決を下した。
その数週間後の4月29日、米国から海を渡ってきた5人のキリスト教司教が、東京の会場で100名を超える家庭連合の二世信者たちと一堂に会した。
日本の信徒たちにとって、それは自分たちが孤立していないことを世界に示す瞬間となった。
会場では、日米の二世信者と専門家たちが集うトークセッションが開かれた。海を渡ってきた5人の司教、日本側の有識者、そして両国の二世たちが一つの会場に集まり、日本で起きていることについて率直に語り合った。
司教たちの励ましの言葉は、聖書と、国境を越えた信徒の連帯に根ざしていた。
パネルはUPFジャパンのタマミ氏が司会を務め、日本側パネリストとして魚谷事務総長と中山達樹弁護士が登壇した。
米国の聖職者は以下の通り。
ロナルド・シェリー司教（リコンシリエーション・アポストリック・ミニストリーズ／ネバダ州ラスベガス）
エイブラム・ディクソン司教（エイブラム・ディクソン・ミニストリー／テキサス州ヒューストン）
エドワード・バーネット司教（グレーター・グレース・ファミリー・ミニストリー／ワシントンD.C.）
レオナルド・デュー司教（スター・オブ・ホープ・ミッショナリー・バプテスト教会／カリフォルニア州サンフランシスコ・ベイエリア）
ジョン・ワッツ司教（キングダム・ライフ・チャーチ・アポストリック／メリーランド州ボルチモア）
魚谷事務総長が訪問団の聖職者たちに順次質問を投げかけ、中山弁護士は日本の聴衆のために法的な背景を説明した。
最初に発言したロナルド・トーマス司教は、解散の知らせは衝撃だったが、祈りの中で聖書の長い歴史に根ざした別の読み方へと導かれたと語った。
「旧約聖書と新約聖書を通して、迫害が来るとき、その後には必ず約束が続いてきました。」
それこそが、自分が東京に来た理由だと彼は述べた。嘆くために来たのではなく、今起きていることを通して自分自身がどう成長できるかを問うために来たのだと語り、その働きは「米国のためでも、日本のためでもなく、神の国のためのものです」と付け加えた。
教会の閉鎖について発言したエイブラム・ディクソン司教は、最も憂慮すべき点は、判決が下される前にすでに実務的な準備が完了していたことだと指摘した。
彼は日本の信徒たちに圧しかかる現実的な重み、つまり来月の支払いをどうするか分からないという各教会の状況を認めつつ、その答えを摂理的な言葉で語った。
「神は生きておられ、神はその子らのためにすべてを備えておられます。神は日本を守っておられます。皆さんは必ず栄えます。大丈夫です。」
エドワード・バーネット司教は、信教の自由を聖書的な土台にまで遡り、モーセが民を奴隷状態から導き出したこと、神のみを礼拝せよという戒め、そしてイエスが同じ命令を再確認したことを振り返った。
「自由はあらゆる領域を超越します。国家は身体を拘束することはできます。魂を揺さぶることもできます。しかし、いかなる人間の力も魂そのものに触れることはできません。それこそが礼拝の自由であると、私は信じています。」
彼の言葉は、日本のケースに関する国際的な見解とも響き合った。国連自由権規約委員会は、日本に対し「公共の福祉」を理由に信教の自由を制限しないよう勧告しており、市民的及び政治的権利に関する国際規約第18条はそのような制限を認めていないと指摘している。
カリフォルニア州ベイエリアから来日したレオナルド・デュー司教は、まず日本のメンバーの歓迎に感謝を述べた後、キリスト教の聖職者たちをしばしば分け隔てる問いへと話を進めた。教派の違いの意味についてである。
訪問団の司教たちはそれぞれ異なる伝統に属していると彼は認めた。外から見れば、その分かれた枝々は「社交クラブ」のようにすら映るかもしれない。しかし、それらの枝はすべて同じ一つの体に帰っていくのだと彼は強調した。「私たちは皆一つです。」
日本の信徒への最後の励ましのメッセージで、彼はその確信をさらに深く語った。
「私たちは一つです。イエスへの信仰を通して、私たちは一つです。そして、私たちは同じ使命を共有しています。御霊と真理の中で、私たちはすべての栄光を神に捧げ、ここで日本の人々と共に神を讃え、高く掲げることによって、私たちは一つなのです。」
法人としての教会そのものが解体されようとしている中で、信徒の共同体が持つ意味とは何か。この問いに対して、ジョン・ワッツ司教は、この日の対談で最も多く引用される一節を語った。
「信徒の共同体とは場所ではありません。あなたがどこへ行こうとも、あなた自身が教会なのです。」
二世の信徒たちに直接語りかけながら、彼はこう続けた。
「この困難な時にあって、あなた方のアイデンティティはキリストご自身です。場所が教会なのではありません。あなた方自身が、神の宿られる教会なのです。」
彼はまた、礼拝の自由を、信徒たちが「恥じることなく神の御前に立つ」ことを可能にする神から与えられた権利であると述べ、会場に集まった若者たちの姿を「神は日本で働いておられ、神は生きておられる」ことの証だと指摘した。
中山弁護士は、会場からの法的な質問に答えた。
完全な解散の後、日本で同じ名称のもとに再出発することは非常に難しいだろうと彼は述べた。しかし、宗教団体に国家への登録を強制する法律は日本には存在せず、信徒たちには集会の権利が残されている。
これまで日本国内でも裁判の過程における不当性を一貫して訴え続けてきた。しかし日本の裁判所のみで判決を覆すことは極めて難しいのが現状である。それゆえに、国際的な支援が不可欠なのだと彼は語った。
彼はまた、裁判所自身が、2022年に安倍晋三元首相を暗殺したとして有罪判決を受けた山上徹也の行動について、「家庭連合の解散とは何の関係もない」と認めた点を強調した。
家庭連合の世界宣教本部によれば、米国国務省は、日本が解散の根拠として民事訴訟を用いていることは、自由民主主義国家の常識と規範に反すると指摘している。
エドワード・バーネット司教は、また家庭連合の指導者である韓鶴子総裁に話を向けた
文鮮明師の時代から受け継がれてきた揺るぎない姿勢を称えた後、彼は韓総裁の韓国における進行中の裁判に話題を移した。その裁判を通じて見せている韓総裁の態度と姿は、二世の信徒たちにより深い信仰と神とのより大きな一体への道を示し、彼らに勇気を与えていると彼は語った。
「お母様（韓総裁）は揺るぎません。私も揺るぎません。この時にあって、私たちは諦めません。沈黙しません。エリコの城壁が崩れるその時まで、私たち一人ひとりが叫び続けるのです。」
彼は韓総裁のノーベル平和賞へのノミネートに触れ、日本の信徒たちと米国の聖職者たちが一つとなって立つ姿を目にして深く感動したと述べた。
会場の参加者の中には、「HELP」や「HELP OUR CHURCH」と書かれたTシャツや手持ちのプラカードを掲げる姿が随所に見られた。米国の二世信徒たちは、日本の家庭連合への継続的な支援活動の一環として、こうしたメッセージをTシャツやプラカードに掲げて活動してきた。
フォーラムの最後に、ワッツ司教はこの「help」という単語のラテン語の語源を、嵐の前に船員たちが積荷を縛るために用いる「綱」を意味する語へと辿った。
「私たちは散らされません。神は私たちの積荷を一つに縛っておられます。多くの国々の綱で、世界の綱で。」

司教たちはその後、法的な障害にかかわらず、神の御業は、日本の信徒らと共に、続いていくことを宣言した。

世界中の家庭連合の信徒たちは、日本の兄弟姉妹を見守り、この試練を乗り越えられるよう支援を送っている。
5人の米国キリスト教司教による異例の東京訪問は、家庭連合の解散がもはや国内の宗教法人の問題としてではなく、国境を越えた信教の自由の問題として捉えられていることを示している。
中山弁護士が繰り返し不可欠だと語った「国際的な支援」は、この日、東京の会場の中で目に見える形を取った。
日米二世と有識者のトークセッション全編動画：https://www.youtube.com/live/YgmIoCEpPSk?si=_2ATENpzL8ln5Dkj

title: The Witness Korea Built Its Religious Crackdown On Has a Diary Problem
previewText: President Lee Jae Myung has spent five months demanding the power to dissolve the Family Federation. The 23rd hearing of Hak Ja Han's trial shows what his case is built on: a witness whose own diary

blocks.text: President Lee Jae Myung has spent five months demanding the power to dissolve the Family Federation. The 23rd hearing of Hak Ja Han's trial shows what his case is built on: a witness whose own diary places him at home, asleep, when his most consequential testimony says he was at work.
— President Donald Trump, Truth Social, August 25, 2025, hours before meeting President Lee Jae Myung at the White House
On April 28, the Seoul Central District Court did two things in the same afternoon.

It extended the medical detention suspension of Hak Ja Han, 82, the president of the Family Federation for World Peace and Unification, to 2 p.m. on May 30. And it heard testimony establishing that the morning briefing on which the prosecution's central theory of her direct involvement depends did not happen. The witness whose testimony is the foundation of that theory, Yoon Young-ho, was at home asleep when he says he was at work.

The court accepted defense submissions on Han's medical condition and approved the extension hours after the hearing concluded. The combined record, as introduced into evidence in open court at the 23rd hearing of the trial, undermines four of the prosecution's load-bearing factual claims.

The case against Han is the showcase for a presidential demand that has reshaped the South Korean conversation about religious freedom over the past five months. President Lee Jae Myung has used the case to argue, from the cabinet table and the New Year's press conference, that the South Korean government should hold the legal authority to dissolve religious corporations administratively. The 23rd hearing places that demand on a new footing.
The President Who Asked for Power to Dissolve a Religion

On December 2, 2025, in a cabinet meeting at the presidential office in Yongsan, President Lee instructed Minister of Government Legislation Cho Won-cheol to review legal grounds for dissolving religious corporations. Lee cited Japan's dissolution order against the Family Federation as a precedent. He avoided naming the Family Federation, referring instead to "a religious group" that had "intervened in politics."

A week later, on December 9, Lee returned to the topic and pressed Cho on the floor of the cabinet meeting. The Korea Herald reported the exchange in detail. Cho confirmed that under Article 38 of the Civil Act, a court may order dissolution where a religious organization has been "persistently and systematically engaged in significant illegal activities."

Lee then asked which government body holds the authority to revoke a religious organization's permit, and what becomes of the assets of dissolved organizations. Cho's answer: assets revert to the state if there are no bylaws to direct otherwise.

Lee's framing at the December 9 cabinet meeting, as quoted by Korea Herald: "Individuals are sanctioned when they commit crimes or antisocial behavior. Likewise, if a corporation commits acts that violate the Constitution and the law, it should be dissolved."

At his January 21, 2026 New Year's press conference, Lee escalated. He warned that "religious interference in politics" leads to "national downfall," likening it to armed rebellion. He said: "The current level of punishment seems far too weak." He urged that the ongoing investigations into the Family Federation and Shincheonji be used as an "opportunity" to "root out" religious involvement in politics entirely.

Newt Gingrich, on Fox News, characterized the trajectory in stark terms: "We are watching our ally in South Korea, which is headed up by basically a pro-communist government, literally and methodically destroying Christianity."

A Korea Times editorial of December 10, 2025, examining Lee's renewed dissolution demand, observed flatly: "There is no convincing evidence that the church has effectively influenced political affairs through its relationships with influential politicians." The editorial added that "the dissolution of a religious organization is an extreme measure that should only be taken when the harm it poses is both demonstrable and severe."
The harm has not been demonstrated. The legal foundation Lee has been demanding rests, in the case the prosecution has selected as its showcase, on the testimony of one man.
The Headlines That Were Already Conclusions

The framing of the Han Hak Ja case did not originate in Western newsrooms. It originated in Seoul. Korean legacy media has presented the special counsel's filings as the case itself, and Yonhap (연합뉴스), the South Korean wire service that supplies most international coverage of Korean politics, has produced the daily wire copy that anchored coverage at home and abroad.

The pattern is well-documented and not unique to this case. South Korean legacy media presents complex political-legal stories from a single editorial direction, most often aligned with whichever coalition holds executive power. Under the Lee administration, the editorial direction has placed the Family Federation outside the protected category of "religion" and inside the actionable category of "political-religious corruption." Once a story sits inside that category, the Korean coverage tends to track the prosecution's filings without independent challenge.

The framing is visible in the wire copy and in the headline labels. Heraldcorp (헤럴드경제), in continuing coverage of the proceedings, has used the standing label "'불법 청탁' 한학자 통일교 총재" ("'Illegal solicitation' Han Hak Ja, Unification Church president") in headline after headline, placing the disputed charge as a settled descriptor of the defendant.

Newstapa (뉴스타파), the left-leaning investigative outlet that broke the Dior-bag story against former first lady Kim Keon Hee, conducted a covert infiltration of a Family Federation prayer gathering on the day of Han's arrest hearing and produced a video framing the religious organization's worship practices as cultic spectacle.

Hankyoreh, MBC, KBS, and the major dailies tracked the prosecution's filings as the substance of the case. The Korean coverage that did surface contradicting evidence, including Lee Se-hyun's News1 reporting on the TM Special Report dating dispute, did not break out of the trade-press circuit. The political talk shows did not pick it up. The dailies did not lead with it.

The international coverage took the Korean wire copy as the case. Western newsrooms, working under deadline and without independent Korean-language reporting capacity, picked up the Yonhap feed and tracked it.
CNN, September 22, 2025: "Unification Church leader arrested in bribery case involving former South Korean president's wife."

Al Jazeera, September 23: "Unification Church leader arrested in S Korean ex-first lady bribery case," with paragraphs noting that followers are referred to "disparagingly" as "Moonies" and that the church has "a cult-like culture."

The South China Morning Post, September 2: "South Korea bribery probe targets Unification Church's 'business-religion complex'." Korea Herald on October 10, reporting the indictment as the consequence of Han's "collusion with a former church official surnamed Yun."
Korea Times on December 16, framing the case as a "major bribery and lobbying scandal."
A complex case with contested evidence, multiple defendants, and shifting witness testimony became a single English headline. The single English headline became the story the world knows.

Through the eight months between Han's arrest and the 23rd hearing, the substantive contents of the indictment, including the Geonjin Beopsa intermediary, the Graff necklace, the Chanel bags, the 100 million won in alleged political funds, and the morning briefing at which Han was supposedly informed and consented, were reported as factual elements of the case rather than as contested allegations resting on a single insider's account. The Family Federation's denials were noted.
The defense's October 2, 2025 statement to the court that "most of Yoon's testimony was untrue" appeared in financial trade press coverage and was not pursued.
The framing was the conclusion. The trial was the formality.
What the Trial Record Established on April 28
The 23rd trial hearing introduced into evidence the documentary record that contradicts the central elements of Yoon's account.
The morning briefing of March 10, 2022 did not occur.

Yoon has testified that on the day after the South Korean presidential election he received a call at 7 a.m. from Rep. Kweon Seong-dong of the People Power Party during a regular morning briefing with Han, and that he conveyed thanks from the lawmaker to her. Yoon's own diary, submitted to the court, records that he woke up at 9:30 a.m. that morning. Cheonjeonggung gate access logs, also submitted, show Yoon entered the facility at 9:42 a.m. He cannot have received a call at 7 a.m. inside a meeting that was not occurring inside a building he had not yet entered. The "thank-you" call, the founding piece of the prosecution's chain-of-knowledge theory against Han, did not happen as Yoon described it.
The "special reports" Yoon described as the conduit for delivering political fund details to Han were not seen by the staff present at the meetings.

Witnesses testified at the April 28 hearing that they never observed printed budget documents at any briefing. They testified that the A3-format presentation boards used at such meetings carried event flow diagrams and visual references, not financial details. The documents the prosecution has cited as evidence of Han's direct knowledge of political fund delivery were not, by the testimony of the people who attended the meetings, ever in the meetings.
Han issued no instruction to deliver funds to Jeon Seong-bae,

the figure identified in court documents by the Buddhist alias Geonjin Beopsa and alleged to have served as the intermediary in the gifting to former first lady Kim Keon Hee. Witnesses testified she issued no instruction to purchase the Graff necklace. The witnesses described Han's longstanding practice of selecting gifts personally and not delegating such purchases. The instructions Yoon attributes to her were not, on the testimony of the staff who would have received and executed them, ever issued.
The March 2, 2022 Lotte Hotel assembly was not a political endorsement event.

It was a thank-you gathering organized by the hotel for the recently concluded World Summit conference. Witnesses testified that Han's remarks at the event contained no political endorsement and no reference to specific candidates.

Each of these four contradictions touches a load-bearing element of the prosecution's theory of Han's direct knowledge and direction. Each was published as fact across the Korean and international press. Each was cited, in some form, in the political demand that the religious organization be dissolved.
The Document Dated 2022 That Wasn't Made Until 2023

The contradictions in Yoon's testimony are not the only credibility problem in the prosecution's case file. The TM Special Report, "TM" referring to True Mother, the title used for Han within the Family Federation, is the document the special counsel has cited as the conduit through which political fund details were briefed to Han at the morning meetings. It is the document at the center of Yoon's most consequential testimony. The special counsel submitted it into evidence as a 2022 document. The defense has established that it was created in 2023.

Reporter Lee Se-hyun documented the dispute in News1 on December 28, 2025, under the headline "'Kweon Seong-dong 100 million won' Unification Church 'TM Special Report' truth dispute… 'Date is wrong' vs 'Can be proven'." Han's defense argued that the special prosecutor had distorted the facts by dating the document to 2022. The special counsel's response, as reported by News1: the date had been "identified by comparison with other materials." The document's own provenance was not what the prosecution offered as the basis for its 2022 dating. Other materials were.

The displacement of one year is not a clerical issue. The 2022 dating is what places the TM Special Report inside the morning briefing cycle of the 2022 presidential election period. The 2023 dating places it after the events the prosecution alleges Han knew about and approved through that document. A briefing document Yoon could not have used to brief Han in 2022 cannot establish that Han was briefed in 2022.

The connection to the diary problem is direct. Yoon has testified that he used the TM Special Report to brief Han on the morning of March 10, 2022. His diary places him at home asleep that morning. The document he says he used did not exist until 2023. The prosecution has continued, in the four months between the News1 report and the April 28 hearing, to base its theory of Han's direct involvement on the document and the meeting that the trial record now indicates were not where the prosecution placed them.
What Was Already Visible
The contradictions placed on the trial record on April 28 were not unforeseeable. Yoon himself had begun recanting parts of his earlier testimony before the 23rd hearing.

In late 2025, after the special police investigation into the broader bribery probe expanded, Yoon told investigators he "never gave money to anyone he didn't know personally." He testified at trial that he had also approached, and in some cases financially supported, Democratic Party politicians, naming as many as 15 DPK figures who he said may have received support through donations or bulk book purchases. Rep. Chun Jae-soo, the incumbent DPK lawmaker rumored to be implicated, then serving as Minister of Oceans and Fisheries, announced his intention to resign in December 2025 amid the disclosures.

The story Yoon had told the special counsel, that the Family Federation had directed funds to a single conservative party in exchange for political favors, did not survive his own subsequent testimony. The story he had told about the morning briefing did not survive his own diary.

The pattern by which the prosecution's case was assembled, transmitted, and converted into a presidential demand for institutional dissolution did not pause to test the witness against the documentary record. The 23rd hearing did.
A Pattern of Pressure Beyond One Religion
The prosecution of Han proceeds inside a wider pattern of state action against religious organizations in South Korea over the past year.

Sarang Jeil Church, headed by evangelical preacher Jun Kwang-hoon, who led protests in support of former President Yoon Suk Yeol, was raided by Seoul police in August 2025. Jun has been forbidden from leaving South Korea pending investigations. Yoido Full Gospel Church, one of the largest Pentecostal congregations in the world, was raided by special prosecutors. Pastor Son Hyun-bo of Segeroh Presbyterian Church in Busan was arrested in 2025, convicted on charges of using his religious standing to influence elections, and released in early 2026 on a suspended six-month sentence. Shincheonji has been named alongside the Family Federation by President Lee in his calls to "root out" religious involvement in politics.

The question Trump posted before the August 25, 2025 White House meeting was not asked in a vacuum. He told reporters at the White House the same morning that he had heard "very vicious" raids on churches were occurring under the new government and that he would "get to the bottom of it" with Lee. Lee's staff feared the Oval Office meeting would devolve into a "Zelenskyy moment," as Lee himself later acknowledged. Former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo described the Han prosecution as "lawfare" and called the broader trend "intensifying assaults on religious liberty" that betrayed South Korea's democratic principles.

The Tokyo High Court on March 4, 2026 upheld the dissolution order against the Family Federation in Japan, the most consequential religious-organization dissolution ruling in postwar Japanese law. Lee has cited the Japanese precedent in his cabinet demands. The Tokyo case advanced on aggregated civil claims and former-member testimony rather than criminal conviction. The Seoul case has produced no criminal conviction of Han and now faces the documented contradiction of its central cooperating witness.
The Cell and the Calendar

Han has been held in a 70-square-foot solitary cell at the Seoul Detention Center since September 22, 2025. She appeared at her first trial hearing in December in a wheelchair. She appeared for special counsel questioning while still recovering from a heart procedure. She has been granted four medical detention suspensions: three days in November 2025 for eye surgery; ten days in February 2026, including treatment for injuries sustained in a fall during her first release; an extended suspension in March 2026 for shoulder surgery; and the April 28 ruling extending the current suspension by one month to May 30.

She is 82 years old. The court has accepted, in each instance, that her medical condition makes continued confinement inappropriate. The prosecution has continued, in each instance, to argue against extension.

The Family Federation observes May 2 as the 66th anniversary of the Holy Wedding of its founders and the first anniversary of the entrance ceremony at the Cheonwon Palace. In a message conveyed on April 25 to Family Federation members in Japan, where the organization faces dissolution, Han addressed her followers in that jurisdiction: "I trust the Japanese members. I know the loyal heart and devotion that follow even through difficulty."
What the Record Now Shows

The April 28 hearing did not vindicate Han. It introduced sworn testimony and documentary evidence that contradicts the testimony on which the prosecution's theory of her direct involvement depends. That distinction matters in a criminal trial, where the burden of proof rests with the prosecution. It matters in a public square, where the framing of the case has carried political consequences far beyond the courtroom.

The president continues to demand the legal authority to dissolve the religious organization to which the trial is directed. The witness on whose account that authority is being demanded has been placed by the documentary record outside the room where his most consequential testimony situates him.

The trial continues at the Seoul Central District Court. The reporting that built the case as a settled corruption story has not yet caught up to the trial record that built the case as something else.

title: Faith Beyond Borders: US Bishops Stand with Japan’s Family Federation
previewText:
When a nation erases a religion as a legal entity, what do believers across the sea have to say?
On 4 March 2026, the Tokyo High Court issued a ruling that erased the Family Federation for World Peace

blocks.text: When a nation erases a religion as a legal entity, what do believers across the sea have to say?
On 4 March 2026, the Tokyo High Court issued a ruling that erased the Family Federation for World Peace and Unification as a legal entity.

A few weeks later, in April, five Christian Bishops who had crossed the ocean from the United States gathered with more than one hundred second-generation Family Federation believers at a venue in Tokyo.
For the Japanese believers, it became a moment that showed the world they were not standing alone.

The venue hosted a talk session bringing together second-generation believers from Japan and the United States alongside subject-matter experts. The five Bishops who had crossed the ocean, Japanese experts, and second-generation believers from both countries gathered in one room to speak openly about what was unfolding in Japan.
The Bishops grounded their message of encouragement in scripture and in the solidarity of believers across borders.
The panel was moderated by Tamami of UPF Japan and featured Secretary-General Uotani and attorney Nakayama Tatsuki as the Japanese panelists.
The American clergy:
Bishop Ronald Thomas — Reconciliation Apostolic Ministries, Las Vegas, Nevada
Bishop Abram Dixon — Abram Dixon Ministry, Houston, Texas
Bishop Edward Barnett — Greater Grace Family Ministry, Washington D.C.
Bishop Leonard Dew — Star of Hope Missionary Baptist Church, San Francisco Bay Area, California
Bishop John Watts — Kingdom Life Church Apostolic, Baltimore, Maryland
Uotani put questions to the visiting clergy in turn, while Nakayama provided legal context for a Japanese audience following via livestream.
Bishop Ronald Thomas, the first to speak, said the dissolution news had come as a shock, but that prayer had brought him to a different reading rooted in the long arc of scripture.
"Throughout the Old Testament and the New Testament, when persecution has come, it has always been followed by promise."

That, he said, was why he was in Tokyo at all. He had come not to mourn but to ask how he himself could grow through what was unfolding, and the work, he added, was "not for America, not for Japan, but for the Kingdom of God."
Bishop Abram Dixon, addressing the church closures, said the most troubling element was that operational preparations had clearly been completed before the court ruled.
He acknowledged the practical weight pressing on Japanese believers — congregations now uncertain how next month's bills would be paid — but framed the answer in providential terms.
"God is alive, and God has prepared everything for His children. God is protecting Japan. You will all flourish. It will be alright."

Bishop Edward Barnett. traced religious freedom to its biblical foundations, recalling Moses leading his people out of slavery, the commandment that God alone be worshipped, and Jesus reaffirming that same instruction.
"Freedom transcends every domain. The state can constrain the body. It can agitate the soul. But what no human power can do is touch the soul itself. That, I believe, is the freedom of worship."

His framing echoed broader international observations on the Japan case. The United Nations Human Rights Committee has recommended that Japan refrain from restricting religious freedom on the basis of "public welfare," noting that Article 18 of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights does not allow such restrictions.

Bishop Leonard Dew, who had traveled from the California Bay Area, opened by thanking the Japanese members for the welcome before turning to a question that often divides Christian clergy: the meaning of denominational difference.

The visiting pastors all belonged to different traditions, he acknowledged, and from the outside the resulting branches could even resemble a "social club." But the branches, he insisted, all returned to the same body. "We are all one."
In his closing message of encouragement to the Japanese members, he pressed the same conviction further.

"We are one. We are one through faith in Jesus. And we share the same mission. Through Spirit and truth, we offer all glory to God, and by lifting up and praising God together with the people of Japan here, we are one."
Bishop John Watts, asked about the meaning of a community of believers when the corporate church itself was being dismantled, offered the panel's most quoted line.
"The community of believers is not a place. Wherever you go, you yourself are the church."
Addressing the second-generation members directly, he continued:
"In this difficult time, your identity is Christ Himself. A place is not the church. You yourselves are the church where God dwells."

He also described the freedom of worship as a God-given right that allowed believers to "stand before God without shame," and pointed to the youth turnout in the room as evidence that "God is at work in Japan, and God is alive."
Attorney Nakayama answered legal questions from the floor.

Restarting under the same name in Japan after a full dissolution would be very difficult, he said, but no Japanese law forces religious organisations to register with the state, and believers retain the right to assemble.
Overturning the ruling through Japanese courts alone would also be very difficult, which made international support essential.

He highlighted that the court itself had held that the actions of Yamagami Tetsuya, the man convicted of assassinating former Prime Minister Abe Shinzo in 2022, "have no relation whatsoever to the dissolution of the Family Federation."

The U.S. State Department has noted that Japan's use of civil litigation to justify dissolution contradicts the common sense and norms of liberal democratic nations, according to the Family Federation's world mission headquarters.
Bishop Edward Barnett turned to Dr. Hak Ja Han, the Family Federation's leader.

He praised the unshaken posture carried through from the days of Rev. Sun Myung Moon, then turned to Han's own ongoing trial in South Korea. Her composure throughout that trial, he said, was showing the second-generation members the path toward deeper faith and greater unity with God — and giving them courage.
"Mother (Dr. Han) is unshaken. I am unshaken. In this time, we will not give up. We will not stay silent. We will keep crying out, every last one of us, until the walls of Jericho fall."
He noted Dr. Han's nomination for the Nobel Peace Prize and said he had been deeply moved to see Japanese believers and American clergy standing as one.

Among the participants in the venue, T-shirts and handheld signs reading "HELP" or "HELP OUR CHURCH" were visible throughout the room. American second-generation believers have been carrying these messages on T-shirts and signs as part of an ongoing campaign of support for Japan's Family Federation.
At the close of the forum, Bishop Watts traced the Latin root of that same word "help" to a term meaning rope, the kind sailors use to bind cargo before a storm.
"We are not going to be scattered. God is binding our cargo together, with the ropes of many nations and the ropes of the world."

The Bishops, together with the Japanese believers, declared that the work of God in Japan would continue regardless of legal obstacles. Members of the Family Federation around the world are watching over their brothers and sisters in Japan, sending their support so that they may overcome this trial.
The pastors then declared together with the Japanese members that the work of God would continue in Japan regardless of legal obstacles.

The unusual visit of five US Christian Bishops to Tokyo signals that the dissolution of the Family Federation is no longer being seen as a domestic religious-corporation matter, but as a question of religious freedom that crosses borders.
The "international support" that attorney Nakayama repeatedly described as essential had, on this day, taken visible form inside the Tokyo venue.
Talk Session Between Japanese and American Second Generation and Experts Full Video : https://www.youtube.com/live/YgmIoCEpPSk?si=_2ATENpzL8ln5Dkj

title: Suspension of Detention Extension and Trial Progress from FFWPU HQ
previewText:
This is a translation of the internal memo from FFWPU HQ originally written in Korean.
Dear beloved family members,
To all family members who are praying with concern for True Mother’s health and

blocks.text: This is a translation of the internal memo from FFWPU HQ originally written in Korean.
Dear beloved family members,
To all family members who are praying with concern for True Mother’s health and well-being, I offer my heartfelt gratitude and comfort.
I would like to briefly report on the recent developments that many of you have been curious about.
Extension of the Suspension of Detention Period
As reported in the media, True Mother’s suspension of detention has been extended until 2:00 PM on May 30.

Before the April 28 court session, the judicial panel confirmed True Mother’s medical condition and expressed their intention that if treatment was not yet complete, an extension should be requested. In response, the defense team immediately submitted the prepared extension application.

The day before, on April 27, the defense submitted an additional opinion for the bail application, and on April 28, they also submitted a written statement explaining her absence. The documents detailed the progress of True Mother’s shoulder surgery and the opinions of the consulting specialists, clearly outlining the necessary future treatment and rehabilitation process, while earnestly conveying serious health concerns that could arise if she were returned to detention.

As a result, right after the court session at around 6:00 PM, the judicial panel approved a one-month extension of the suspension of detention. This is a gracious blessing from Heaven allowing True Mother to focus fully on her treatment, and a precious outcome achieved through the sincere devotion and prayers of all family members.
23rd Court Session: The Truth Revealed and False Claims Exposed

In this court session, the malicious claims that had been raised were proven groundless one by one through testimony and evidence. In particular, decisive testimonies emerged that proved the falsehood of Yoon Young-ho’s claims.

First, it was confirmed that the so-called “morning assembly” and “gratitude phone call” on March 10, 2022, never took place. Yoon Young-ho had claimed that on the morning after the presidential election, at 7:00 AM during the morning assembly, he received a call from Rep. Kwon Seong-dong and conveyed gratitude to Mother. However, multiple testimonies and records confirmed that no morning assembly was held that day. Yoon Young-ho’s own diary showed he woke up at 9:30 AM, clearly proving his claim of receiving a call at 7:00 AM was false. Furthermore, Cheon Jeong Gung gate access records showing that Yoon Young-ho entered at 9:42 AM on March 10 were additionally submitted as evidence, raising further doubts about the credibility of his claims.

Second, regarding the “Special Report” document that has been repeatedly cited as key evidence, a witness testified, “I have never seen him bring and report anything during the morning assembly.” Contrary to claims that important matters including the budget were reported on A3 printed boards, the witness stated, “I have never seen any printed documents with detailed information such as the budget. The A3 boards used for reporting only contained event routes and images, and had nothing to do with the budget.”

Third, testimonies emerged refuting claims related to gifts and fund execution. There was no directive from True Mother regarding payments to the so-called “Geonjin Beopsa” or the purchase of specific luxury items (such as a Graff necklace). The witness stated, “I have never heard such instructions from Mother,” and added, “Mother always chooses gifts herself and never asks others to purchase them on her behalf,” thereby confirming that True Mother gave no illegal directives.

Fourth, it was revealed that the special gathering, which had been claimed to show support for a particular political party, was in fact unrelated to politics. The special event held at Lotte Hotel on March 2, 2022, was prepared by the hotel as a gesture of gratitude for the World Summit. Testimony confirmed that True Mother’s message that day had nothing to do with supporting any specific party or politician.

In this way, through the court proceedings, various facts are being confirmed one by one, and the trial is progressing based on concrete evidence and testimony. What is important is that the truth always reveals itself. Rather than anxiously judging the outcome, we must calmly watch as everything becomes clearly revealed through the judicial process.
A Spring-Filled Holy Marriage Anniversary
Dear beloved family members,

True Mother has a special love for spring. It is because this is the season of True Parents’ Holy Marriage Anniversary. May 2 is a meaningful day that marks the 66th Holy Marriage Anniversary and the 1st anniversary of the Cheon Won Gung entrance ceremony. We are grateful that we can celebrate this precious day during the extended period of suspension of detention. Although it is not complete freedom, we hope this news brings you small comfort and relief.

On April 25, True Mother expressed her concern for the Japanese family members, saying, “I trust the Japanese family. I know their faithful hearts and devotion as they follow even in the midst of difficulties. I love you all.” Even in hardship, she does not worry about herself but seeks only to comfort and encourage us. We could deeply feel True Mother’s great love.

True Mother always looks first to the Will of Heavenly Parent in any situation and continues to guide us. As we approach the Holy Marriage Anniversary, I pray that all family members will renew the mission contained in the Words and deeply engrave True Mother’s love and heart in your hearts, becoming true children of Heaven. Aju.

title: 미국 신자 100여 명 일본 방문 ー가정연합 해산 사태 속, 국경 넘은 결속
previewText:
미국 전역에서 100명이 넘는 가정연합 2세 멤버와 목회자들이 약 2주 만에 기획하고 도쿄로 모였다.
세계평화통일가정연합(가정연합) 멤버 100여 명이 미국 각지에서 도쿄로 건너와, 해산 명령의 여파를 마주하고

blocks.text: 미국 전역에서 100명이 넘는 가정연합 2세 멤버와 목회자들이 약 2주 만에 기획하고 도쿄로 모였다.
세계평화통일가정연합(가정연합) 멤버 100여 명이 미국 각지에서 도쿄로 건너와, 해산 명령의 여파를 마주하고 있는 일본 신자들과 직접 대면하며 일본의 종교 자유를 지키기 위한 연대의 뜻을 보였다.
약 2주 만에 조직된 이번 방문은 미국의 2세 멤버와 목회자들을 일본 측 2세들과 만나게 한 자리로, 가정연합이 종교법인 자격을 박탈당한 시점에 이루어졌다.
방문의 중심이 된 것은 2026년 4월 29일에 '신자의 인권을 지키는 2세 모임')이 주최한 '한미 2세와 한미 전문가 토크 세션'이었다.
이 세션은 라이브로 송출되었고, 일본 측 3명과 미국 측 3명의 2세 멤버가 처음으로 같은 무대에 마주 앉는 형식으로 진행되었다. "전혀 소통할 시간이 없어서, 지금 처음으로 얼굴을 마주합니다"라고 사회자가 첫머리에 말했다.
일본 측 첫 발언자로 나선 야마나시현 2세 교회장 기쿠타니 씨는 판결 이후의 충격을 먼저 풀어놓았다. 청산인이 예고도 없이 교회 시설로 들어왔다고 한다.
갑자기 청산인이 들어오면서, 작별을 아쉬워할 시간조차 없이 한순간에 교회가 폐쇄되었습니다.
그러면서도 기쿠타니 씨는 해산 절차가 시작된 이후 야마나시에서 예배에 모이는 신도 수가 오히려 3배로 늘었다고 전했다. 그는 이를 호리 마사아키 가정연합 회장의 목회적 리더십, 그리고 지역 신도들 간의 서로를 지탱하는 마음 덕분이라고 돌렸다.
오늘 여러분과 함께 참어머님(한학자 총재)과 하나가 되어 기적을 일으킵시다.
이어서 미국 측에서 일본 측에 솔직한 질문이 던져졌다.
"이런 상황 속에서 일본 멤버들은 어떻게 신앙을 지키고 희망을 잃지 않고 있는지 듣고 싶다."
29세 2세 멤버이며 일하면서 아이를 키우고 있는 구라하시 사야카 씨는, 교회 건물을 잃은 상태에서 이어가는 신앙생활의 결을 들려주었다.
가정연합 해산이라는 사실은 사실이고, 절망은 절망이고, 정말 힘들고, 모두를 쉽게 만날 수 없어서 외롭기도 합니다. 그래도 하나님과의 관계는 변하지 않았고, 지금까지 쌓아온 공동체, 그 관계성도 변하지 않았어요.
그녀는 폐쇄된 교회를 대신해 지역 예배나 야외 예배가 이어지고 있다고 전하며, 하나님과의 종적인 관계와 신도 간의 횡적인 관계야말로 자신의 세대가 지키려 하는 축이라고 말했다.
사회를 맡은 고지마 씨는 구라하시 씨와 매일 유튜브 콘텐츠를 함께 만들고 있는 2세 멤버이기도 하다. "부모님이 교회 해산을 마주하면서도 흔들리지 않고 쓰러지지 않는 모습을 보면서, 우리도 이 소중한 교회를 지켜가고 싶다고 느꼈습니다"라고 말했다.
│ 이 사건 덕분에 일본의 2세들은, 왜 신앙하는지, 왜 하나님을 믿는지를 정말 깊이, 진지하게 생각하게 된 계기를 얻었습니다.
미국 북서부 지역 리저널 디렉터를 맡고 있는 나오키미 씨는, 이번 방문의 동기가 미국 신도들 사이에서 폭넓게 공유되고 있는 마음이라고 전했다. 도쿄에서 태어나 두 살 때 미국으로 건너가 자란 나오키미 씨는, 미국 가정연합 멤버의 약 절반이 일본에 뿌리를 두고 있다고 말했다.
아무리 생각해도 이런 일이 벌어졌다는 것이 받아들여지지 않습니다. 미국의 감각으로 보면 도저히 있을 수 없는 일입니다. 데미안 던클리 회장님의 리더십 아래, 미국 멤버들은 진심으로 일본을 돕고 싶다는 마음으로 이곳에 왔습니다.
미국 가정연합은 약 76개 공동체로 구성되어 있으며, 규모는 10명 정도의 모임에서 약 500명 규모의 교회까지 폭이 있다고 한다.
나오키미 씨는 또한 미국에서 가정연합의 사회적 인지도가 세대에 따라 크게 갈린다는 점도 짚었다. "하나님을 믿는다고 말하는 건 쉽다. 하지만 통일교회의 멤버라고 말하는 건 세대에 따라 어려움이 다르다."
젊은 세대는 교회 자체를 거의 모르는 반면, 윗세대에는 수십 년 전 논란의 이미지가 남아 있다는 것이다.
지난해 인사로 미국에 건너가, 나오키미 씨 아래에서 바이스 리저널 디렉터를 맡고 있는 기쿠치 가즈히데 씨(41세)는, 미국 교회에서 가장 인상적이었던 것은 주일 예배의 열기라고 말했다.
미국에서는 온몸으로 하나님을 찬양합니다. 매주 주일 예배가 끝날 때마다 정말로 행복한 기분이 듭니다.
이어서 그는 미국에서 목사라는 직분에 대한 사회적 존중에 놀란 경험을 소개했다. 미국에 도착한 직후 입국 심사에서 영어 질문을 알아듣지 못해 곤란해하던 기쿠치 씨는, 마지막에 자신 있게 "I am a pastor(저는 목사입니다)"라고 답했다. 그러자 심사관은 그대로 통과시켜 주었다고 한다. "정말로 신뢰받고 존중받는 직업이라는 걸 느꼈습니다."
사회자 고지마 씨는 그 대비를 솔직하게 말로 옮겼다. "일본에서 지금 '저는 목사입니다'라고 말하면, 정반대의 일이 벌어지지요."
이 짧은 대화는, 종교적 다양성이 시민사회에 자연스럽게 자리 잡은 미국과, 신앙, 특히 소수 신앙을 공개적으로 표현하기가 점점 더 어려워지고 있는 오늘의 일본 사이의 거리를 또렷이 드러냈다.
댈러스를 거점으로 미국 남부 9개 주를 총괄하고 있는 지닐 플라이슈만 씨는, 이번 방문을 가능하게 한 문화적 토양은 교회의 울타리를 훨씬 넘어선다고 말했다.
미국에는 우리의 신앙만이 아니라 사회 전체에 뿌리내린 훌륭한 자선 문화가 있습니다. 많은 크리스천은 자신의 재산이 늘어나면 교회나 사회에 더 많이 베풀고 싶다고 생각합니다. 사람들은 베푸는 것을 통해 다른 이들을 축복할 수 있다고 느낍니다.
미국 사람들은 돈뿐만 아니라 시간과 노력도 아낌없이 내어놓는 경향이 있고, 바로 그 문화가 있었기에 이번 파견단이 단기간에 성사될 수 있었다.
이번 일본 방문 비용은 전액 약 2주 만에 모인 신도들의 헌금으로 충당되었다.
사회자는 이번 방문이 가진 가시성 그 자체에 의의가 있다고 말했다.
일본의 TV 보도는 2세를, 본인의 의사와 무관하게 예배에 끌려 다니고 헌금을 강요당하는 존재로 일관되게 그려 왔다. 100명이 넘는 미국 2세 멤버가 단기간에 일본을 찾았다는 사실 자체가 그 프레임에 정면으로 반박하고 있는 셈이다.
"다른 종교 단체의 신자들이 다른 나라에서 비행기를 타고 와서 연대를 보여 주는 광경은, 누구도 본 적이 없습니다"라고 구라하시 씨는 덧붙였다.

일본 측 등단자들에게 이번 방문이 갖는 가장 큰 의의는, 그것이 일본의 종교 자유 전반에 미치는 파급에 있다. 일본 사법에 의해 해체되어 가고 있는 종교 공동체를 위해, 바다를 건너와 공개적으로 함께 서는 신자들이 있다는 사실. 이는 가정연합이 일본 방송 미디어가 그려 온 것 같은 고립되고 미움받는 집단이 아니라, 국경을 넘어선 양심의 공동체의 한 부분임을 보여 준다.
이번 행사는, 일본인 한 사람 한 사람이 가지고 있던 가정연합에 대한 이미지를 180도 바꾸어 놓을 것이라고 생각합니다.
등단자들은, 이러한 대중 인식의 변화가 시간을 두고 일본 사회에서 신앙, 특히 소수 종교 단체에서도 신앙을 두려움 없이 떳떳하게 실천할 수 있는 자리를 넓혀 가기를 바란다고 말했다.
한미 2세와 한미 전문가 토크 세션 풀 영상: https://www.youtube.com/live/YgmIoCEpPSk?si=_2ATENpzL8ln5Dkj

title: アメリカ信者100人以上が来日 ー 家庭連合の解散下に国境越えた連帯
previewText: 米国全土から100人を超える家庭連合の二世メンバーと牧師が、わずか約2週間で企画し、東京に駆けつけた。

blocks.text: 米国全土から100人を超える家庭連合の二世メンバーと牧師が、わずか約2週間で企画し、東京に駆けつけた。
世界平和統一家庭連合（家庭連合）のメンバー100人以上が、米国各地から東京に渡り、解散命令の余波と向き合う日本の信者と直接対面し、日本の信教の自由を守るために連帯を示した。約2週間で組織されたこの訪問は、米国の二世メンバーと牧師たちを日本側の二世と引き合わせるもので、家庭連合が宗教法人格を剥奪されたタイミングで実現した。
訪問の中心となったのが、2026年4月29日に「信者の人権を守る二世の会」が主催した**「日米2世と日米有識者のトークセッション」である。同セッションはライブ配信され、日本側3人と米国側3人の二世メンバーが初めて壇上で顔を合わせる形式で進行した
「全くコミュニケーションも取る時間がなく、今初めて顔を合わせました」と司会者は冒頭で述べた。
日本側の口火を切った山梨県の二世教会長、菊谷氏は、判決後の衝撃をまず語った。清算人が予告なく教会施設に入ってきたという。
突然清算人が入ってこられて、全く名残惜しむ時間もないぐらい一気に教会が閉鎖されました。
一方で菊谷氏は、解散手続きが始まって以降、山梨で礼拝に集う信徒の数は3倍に増えたと報告した。彼はこれを、堀雅明・家庭連合会長の牧会的リーダーシップと、地域信徒同士の支え合いに帰した。
今日、皆さんと共にお母様（韓鶴子総裁）と一つになって奇跡を起こしましょう。

そこで米国側から日本側へ率直な質問が投げかけられた。
「このような状況の中で、日本のメンバーたちはどのように信仰を保ち、希望を持ち続けているのか」。
29歳の二世メンバーで、働きながら子供を育てている倉橋さやか氏は、教会施設を失った状態で続く信仰生活の手触りを語った。
家庭連合解散という事実は事実なんですけど、絶望は絶望だし、すごく大変だし、なんかみんなに簡単に会えないので寂しいんですけど。神様との関係は変わらないし、今まで築いてきたコミュニティ、そういう関係性も変わらない。
彼女は、閉鎖された教会の代わりに地域礼拝や野外礼拝が行われていることに触れ、神様との縦の関係と信徒同士の横の関係こそ、自分たちの世代が守ろうとしている軸だと述べた。
司会を務めた小嶌氏は、倉橋氏と毎日YouTube番組を共同制作している二世メンバーでもある。「両親が教会の解散に直面しても、めげない、倒れない姿を見て、私たちもこの大事な教会を守っていきたいと思いました」と語った。
この事件のおかげで、日本の二世たちは、なぜ信仰しているのか、どうして神様を信じているのかを、すごくディープに真剣に考えた機会でした。
舞台の反対側で、米国北西部地区リージョナル・ディレクターを務めるナオキミ氏は、今回の訪問の動機が米国の信徒の間で広く共有されていると語った。東京で生まれ、2歳から米国で育ったナオキミ氏は、米国家庭連合のメンバーの約半数が日本にルーツを持つと述べた。
何回考えても、こういうことが起こったということが受け止められない。米国の感覚から見ると考えられないことが起きている。デミアン・ダンクリー会長のリーダーシップのもと、米国メンバーは本気で日本を支えたいという気持ちでここに来ました。
米国家庭連合は約76のコミュニティで構成され、規模は10名程度の集まりから約500名の教会まで幅があるという。
ナオキミ氏はまた、米国における家庭連合の社会的認知が世代によって大きく分かれることにも触れた。「神様を信じていると言うのは簡単だ。だが統一教会のメンバーだと言うのは、世代によって難しさが違う」。
若い世代は教会自体をほとんど知らない一方、上の世代には数十年前の論争のイメージが残っているという。
昨年人事で渡米し、ナオキミ氏のもとでバイス・リージョナル・ディレクターを務める菊池和英氏（41歳）は、米国の教会で最も印象的だったのは日曜礼拝の熱量だと語った。
アメリカでは、体全体で神様を賛美している。毎週日曜日の礼拝が終わるとき、本当にハッピーな気持ちになる。
続けて彼は、米国における牧師という立場への社会的尊重に驚かされた経験を紹介した。渡米直後、米国の入国審査で英語の質問が分からず困っていた菊池氏は、最後に自信を持って「I am a pastor（私は牧師です）」と答えた。すると審査官はそのまま通してくれたという。「本当に信頼される、尊敬される職業なのだと感じた」。
司会の小嶌氏はその対比を率直に言葉にした。「日本で今、私は牧師ですなんて言ったら、まったく逆のことが起きてしまう」。
このやりとりは、宗教的多様性が市民社会に組み込まれている米国と、信仰、特に少数派の信仰を公に表明することがますます難しくなっている現在の日本との隔たりを浮き彫りにした。
ダラスを拠点に米国南部9州を統括するジニール・フライシュマン氏は、今回の訪問を可能にした文化的土壌は、教会の枠をはるかに超えていると語った。
米国には、私たちの信仰だけでなく、社会全体に根付く素晴らしい慈善文化がある。多くのクリスチャンは、自分の財産が増えたら、教会や社会にもっと与えたいと考えている。人々は、与えることで他者を祝福できると感じている。
米国人は、お金だけでなく時間や労力も惜しみなく差し出す傾向があり、その文化があったからこそ、今回の派遣団が短期間で成立した
と述べた。今回の訪問費用の全額は、約2週間で集められた信徒からの献金で賄われた。
司会者は、今回の訪問の可視性そのものに意義があると語った。
日本のテレビ報道は二世を、意に反して礼拝に通わされ、献金を強いられている存在として一貫して描いてきた。100人を超える米国の二世メンバーが短期間で来日したという事実そのものが、その枠組みに正面から反論しているのだ。
「他の宗教団体の信徒が、別の国から飛行機で駆けつけて連帯を示す。こんな光景は誰も見たことがない」と倉橋氏は付け加えた。
日本側の登壇者にとって、今回の訪問が持つ最大の意義は、それが日本の信教の自由全体に与える波及効果にある。日本の司法によって解体されつつある宗教共同体に、海を越えてまで公に立ち上がる信徒たちがいるという事実は、家庭連合が日本の放送メディアが描くような孤立した嫌われ者ではなく、国境を越えた良心の共同体の一部であることを示している。
このイベントは、日本人一人一人が持っていた家庭連合のイメージを180度変えると思います。
登壇者たちは、この公衆認識の変化が時間をかけて、日本において信仰、とりわけ少数派の信仰が、恐れることなく堂々と実践できる社会的余地を広げていくことを願うと語った。
日米2世と日米有識者のトークセッション Full Video : https://www.youtube.com/live/YgmIoCEpPSk?si=_2ATENpzL8ln5Dkj

title: 100+ U.S. Believers Arrive in Japan: Solidarity Under Family Federation Dissolution
previewText:
More than 100 second-generation members and pastors arrived in Tokyo, organised in roughly two weeks
More than 100 members of the Family Federation for World Peace and Unification travelled from

blocks.text: More than 100 second-generation members and pastors arrived in Tokyo, organised in roughly two weeks

More than 100 members of the Family Federation for World Peace and Unification travelled from across the United States to Tokyo to stand with Japanese believers and to defend religious freedom in Japan.

The visit, organised in approximately two weeks, brought American second-generation members and pastors face to face with their Japanese counterparts at a moment when Japan's Family Federation has been stripped of its legal status as a religious corporation.

The centrepiece of the visit was a public talk session held on April 29, 2026, hosted by the Second Generation Society to Protect Believers' Human Rights and livestreamed under the title "Talk Session Between Japanese and American Second Generation and Experts."

The format paired three Japanese second-generation members with three American counterparts on stage for the first time. "We had no time to coordinate. We are meeting face to face for the first time right now," the moderator said as the panel opened.

Kikutani, second-generation pastor for Yamanashi prefecture, opened the Japanese side by describing the immediate impact of the court order. He said liquidators had entered church premises with no advance warning.
Liquidators suddenly came in and our churches were closed all at once, with no time even to grieve the loss.

Despite the closures, Kikutani reported that the number of members actively participating in his Yamanashi community has tripled since the dissolution proceedings began. He attributed the resilience to the pastoral leadership of Reverend Hori Masaichi, the organization's Japan president, and to mutual support among local congregations.
Today, with all of you here, let us unite with True Mother (Dr. Hak Ja Han Moon) and create a miracle.
The American members opened the next round with a direct question: how were Japanese members continuing to practice their faith and hold on to hope?
Kurahashi Sayaka, a 29-year-old second-generation member who works while raising a child, described life without church buildings to gather in.

The dissolution of the Family Federation is a fact, and despair is despair. It is hard, and we cannot easily see each other, which is lonely. But our relationship with God has not changed, and the community we have built has not changed.

Members now hold regional and outdoor worship in place of closed church facilities, she said, and the vertical relationship with God and horizontal bonds of community remain the priorities her generation is determined to preserve.

The moderator, Kojima, who co-produces a daily YouTube program with Kurahashi, picked up the thread. "Watching our parents not waver and not fall, even as the church is being dissolved, made us want to protect what they built," she said.
Because of this incident, the second generation in Japan thought very deeply and seriously about why we have faith and why we believe in God.

Naokimi, regional director of the North-Western United States, said the impulse behind the trip was widely shared among American congregations. Born in Tokyo and raised in the United States from age two, he said roughly half of American Family Federation members have Japanese family backgrounds.

However many times I think about what has happened, I cannot wrap my head around it. From an American perspective it is unthinkable. Under President Demian Dunkley's leadership, American members came here genuinely wanting to support Japan.
He described an American Family Federation made up of approximately 76 communities, ranging from groups of around ten to congregations of roughly 500.

Naokimi also described how the church's standing in the United States is generationally split. "It is easy to say I am a believer in God. It is more difficult to say I am a member of the Unification Church, because it depends on the generation," he said.
Younger Americans are largely unfamiliar with the church, while older Americans retain associations from controversies decades earlier.

Kikuchi Kazuhide, 41, who transferred from a Japan leadership post last year to serve as vice regional director under Naokimi, said the most striking difference in American congregations was the energy of Sunday worship.
In America, the whole body praises God. Every week when the Sunday service ends, you feel genuinely happy.

He shared an episode that surprised him about the social respect afforded to clergy in the United States. Stopped at U.S. immigration shortly after his transfer and unable to follow the officer's questions in English, he said with confidence, "I am a pastor." The officer waved him through. "It really is a trusted, respected profession," he said.
The moderator drew the contrast: "In Japan right now, saying you are a pastor would have the opposite effect."

The exchange highlighted what speakers described as the underlying issue: the gap between the United States, where religious diversity is built into civic life, and contemporary Japan, where public expression of faith — particularly minority faith — has become increasingly fraught.
Jinil Fleischman, regional director for nine southern states based in Dallas, Texas, said the culture that made the trip possible extended well beyond the church.

There is a wonderful philanthropic culture across America, not only within our faith. Many Christians feel that if their wealth grows, they want to give more to their church and society. People feel that by giving, they are able to bless others.
Americans give freely in time as well as money to causes they find meaningful, he said, and that culture is what allowed the delegation to assemble at speed.
The full cost of the visit was raised through member donations gathered in roughly two weeks.
The moderator addressed the significance of the visit's public visibility.

Japanese television has consistently depicted second-generation members as reluctant participants coerced into attending services and donating money. The arrival of more than 100 American second-generation members on short notice directly contradicted that frame.
"Other religious groups have never seen anything like this, with people flying in from another country to stand with a community," Kurahashi added.

For the Japanese panelists, the lasting significance of the visit lies in what it offers Japan's wider religious freedom landscape. The presence of foreign believers willing to fly in and publicly stand with a community currently being dismantled by Japanese courts demonstrates that the Family Federation is not the isolated group depicted in domestic broadcast media, but part of a transnational community of conscience.
I believe this event will change the image of the Family Federation that ordinary Japanese hold by 180 degrees.
Speakers said they hoped that shift in public perception would, over time, expand the space in which faith — including minority faith — can be practiced openly and without fear in Japan.
Talk Session Between Japanese and American Second Generation and Experts Full Video : https://www.youtube.com/live/YgmIoCEpPSk?si=_2ATENpzL8ln5Dkj

title: 統一教会:日本の解散命令と国際法 — コンプライアンス宣言の無視
previewText:
原文: Bitter Winter | パトリシア・デュバル著 | 2026年4月27日 | Testimonies Global

2009年の内部改革以降、献金に関して争われた事案はほぼゼロにまで減少した。
 
コンプライアンス宣言

blocks.text:
原文: Bitter Winter | パトリシア・デュバル著 | 2026年4月27日 | Testimonies Global

2009年の内部改革以降、献金に関して争われた事案はほぼゼロにまで減少した。
コンプライアンス宣言
高等裁判所は、新生事件を受けて、教会が2009年2月および3月に、全国の教会指導者に対して2件の内部通知を発出していたと認定した。これらは「コンプライアンス宣言」として知られている。
その内容には、特に次のようなものが含まれていた:
商業的販売活動は宗教法人の目的の範囲外であり、指導者はこれに関与してはならない。
献金を先祖の因縁や業(カルマ)の重荷と結びつけてはならない。
献金は、信徒の自発的意思および経済状況を尊重しなければならない。
献金の受領者は、教会であることを明確にしなければならない。
これらの指示により、民事訴訟件数は劇的に減少した。教会が被告となった民事訴訟のうち、請求が認容された最後の事案は、2014年9月以前の事実に関するものであった。
しかし高裁は、この日付以降に発生したとされる公共の福祉への害を裏付けるため、教会が締結したすべての和解を、不法行為の追加証拠として扱った。
和解について
裁判所は、和解の検討にあたり一つの章を割き、『2010年以降に教団の信者らによって不当な寄附勧誘行為が行われたと認められる事例』という見出しを付した。
裁判所は、訴訟上の勧告によるものか訴訟外で締結されたものかを問わず、すべての和解を教会による不法行為の証拠とみなし、公共の福祉に対する害は重大であり、近年も継続していたと結論づけた。

被告側弁護人が、裁判所が訴訟上の和解を勧告したのは証拠不足によるものであると主張したのに対し、高裁は次のように応じた。
「本件の和解が、訴訟係属中の裁判所の積極的な和解勧告の結果として成立したものであったとしても、そのことは、当該和解が成立したという事実から、教会の信徒が不法行為を構成する不当な献金勧誘行為に及び、よって控訴人が損害賠償責任を負うと認めることが許されないことを意味するものではない。」
これは裁判所による単なる推定にすぎず、不法行為の継続性を立証するものではない。
さらに、教会がメディアによる執拗な犯罪組織化キャンペーンの最中に、世論を鎮めるために締結した訴訟外和解も、不法行為の証拠とは見なせない。
和解書を検討した上で、高裁は「不法行為の可能性を否定できない」と結論づけた。
そして、これは単なる推測に基づくものであるにもかかわらず、裁判所はこれらの和解で対象となった請求全員を「被害者」総数に含め、公共の福祉に対する害の評価における被害額にも算入した。
コンプライアンス宣言の実施措置
裁判所はさらに、コンプライアンス宣言と教会が講じた措置の実効性、ならびに教会の遵守の真意をも問題視した。
裁判所は、宣言の実施措置にて30ページ(英語版56ページから)を割いた。すなわち、宣言の全国の教会組織への配布・掲示、布教活動のガイドラインおよび基準の策定、行動規範、行政通達、牧会規程などの整備で、2009年、2012年、2013年、2014年、2015年、2016年、2018年、2022年、2023年に行われたものである。
過去17年にわたり指針提示にとどまらず実施監督をも目的としていたこれら一連の措置を、裁判所はすべて無効と判断した。その理由として、これらの措置の唯一の目的は民事訴訟と解散命令の回避にあり、不当な資金集めの継続は教会の予算そのものに起因しているため避けがたいとした。
不当な勧誘の根本原因:予算
裁判所は、不当な献金勧誘行為の「根本原因」は、社会的に許容される方法・態様の範囲内では達成し得ない数値目標を設定したことにあると述べた。
教会が被告となった民事訴訟のうち、不法行為が認められた最新の事案の事実関係は、2014年9月にさかのぼる。
過去12年間に立証された不法行為が存在しないにもかかわらず、裁判所は献金額そのものの大きさゆえに、用いられた手法が社会的許容範囲を逸脱したと説明した。

献金額が「社会通念」に照らして過大であるとされたことから、裁判所は、献金者に対して圧力が加えられたに違いないと結論づけた。「さらに、本件の確定判決で認定された不当な献金勧誘行為は、対象者に対し多大な経済的負担および精神的苦痛を課したものであるため、対象者が容易にそのような勧誘に応じるとは考え難く、強い抵抗を示すであろうことは容易に予見できる。したがって、信徒が他者からの働きかけなしに自発的にこのような不当な献金勧誘行為に及んだとは考え難い。社会的許容範囲を超える方法・態様による献金勧誘行為——本件の確定判決で認定された不当な献金勧誘行為——に信徒が及んだのは、社会的許容範囲内の方法・態様による勧誘では達成し得ない献金および物品販売の水準を達成するよう、強い圧力を受けていたためであると推認するのが合理的である。」
このように裁判所は、こうした水準の献金は社会的に許容される手段では達成し得ないと主張するために、単なる推測に依拠したのである。
この立証されていない仮定に基づき、裁判所は次に、教会が年次予算において数値目標を設定し続けていることを批判した。
弁護人は、予算は「献金目標」ではなく、また当該予算は各地方組織における牧会者が前年実績に基づいて作成した予算案によるもので、教会本部が各地方組織に一方的に割り振ったものではないと説明した。
裁判所は、仮にそうであったとしても、「予算に計上された献金収入額が事実上の献金の数値目標として機能していることは否定し難い」と応じた——これは、本件においては地方教会がこの数値目標を設定していたことを意味することになる。
しかし、予算が地方ごとに決定されているのであれば、宗教法人としての教会が「不当な献金勧誘」の「根本原因」ではないということに必然的になる。
次に裁判所は、教会が数値目標に基づいて献金を勧誘すること自体は理論上許容されると認めながらも、数値目標が適正水準で設定されていない場合に問題が生じると判示した。
裁判所は、教会の「予算上の献金収入は、コンプライアンス宣言後の2010年以降も、それ以前とほぼ同水準で推移し続けた」と指摘し、近年も予算達成のために不当な圧力が加えられたに違いないと推認した。
つまり、裁判所による教会への主たる批判は、教会が献金および収入を従前の水準に維持してきたことであり、その金額自体が不当な勧誘行為を引き起こしたとされるのである。
この理由づけに基づいて、裁判所は、教会が「不当な献金勧誘」の再発を防止するための十分な措置(「不当な献金勧誘行為に対する予防措置」)を講じていなかったと結論づけた。
不当な勧誘の根本原因:宗教教義
次に裁判所は、教会が「因縁トーク」を終わらせたと主張しながら、実際には類似の宗教的実践を続けていたと批判した。
裁判所は、献金が捧げられる、韓国で行われる先祖解怨式について論じた。
そして、先祖をその業(カルマ)から解放しなければならないという信仰を、信徒に恐怖心を植え付けて献金を促す手段として描写した。
これもまた精神操作論(theory of mental manipulation)の一例であり、宗教的信仰の領域に対する許されざる介入である。
高裁は、教会がその宗教教義を改めなかった以上、公共の福祉への害が再発するおそれがあると結論づけた。
以上の認定および理由づけに基づき、裁判所は「不当な献金勧誘行為の根本原因は教会自身にある」と認定した。

裁判所は、教会の信徒が、裁判所の認知し得ない不法行為に及び続けたと「推認するのが合理的である」と判示した。「そのような事案の件数および被害額は必ずしも明らかではないが、コンプライアンス宣言後も控訴人が献金収入の予算額(目標額)を従前と同水準で設定し続けたこと(中略)に照らせば、コンプライアンス宣言後も不当な献金勧誘行為の件数および被害額は、それ以前とほぼ同水準にとどまっていたと推認するのが合理的である。」
この未知の被害という仮定によって、教会が公共の福祉に与えたとされる害はさらに膨らんだ。「上述のとおり、教会の信徒による不当な献金勧誘行為がコンプライアンス宣言後も継続し、被害は前述のものに限られないこと、また、当該財産的被害は対象者本人のみならずその家族・親族にも影響を及ぼし得ることを踏まえれば、教会の信徒による不当な献金勧誘行為がもたらした結果は、重大であると言わなければならない。」

高裁はそれゆえに、宗教法人法第81条第1項に定める解散の要件が満たされると結論づけた。「上述のとおり、目的の不当性、態様の悪質性、結果の重大性に照らせば、上述の教団の信者による行為は、宗教法人法第81条第1項第1号に定める『公共の福祉を著しく害することが明らかな行為』に該当するというべきである。」

本記事はBitter Winterに掲載された記事の翻訳である。 クレジットはBitter Winterに帰属する。
https://bitterwinter.org/unification-church-japans-dissolution-order-and-international-law-6-ignoring-the-compliance-declarations/

title: 統一教会:日本の解散命令と国際法ー 解散は必要だったのか
previewText:
原典: Bitter Winter | パトリシア・デュバル著 | 2026年4月25日 | Testimonies Global
裁判所は、教会の解散が「民主的社会において必要」であることを証明できなかった。

blocks.text: 原典: Bitter Winter | パトリシア・デュバル著 | 2026年4月25日 | Testimonies Global
裁判所は、教会の解散が「民主的社会において必要」であることを証明できなかった。
ここで生じる問いは次の通りである。日本が公共の秩序の保護や他者の基本的権利を正当な目的として挙げたとしても、解散命令は民主的社会において必要であり、追求された目的に対して比例的なものであったのか。
解散は民主的社会において必要だったのか
高等裁判所は、教会員による不適切な献金勧誘が「公共の福祉」に「重大な損害」を与えたとして、宗教法人の解散を命じた。
この措置が必要であったかを判断するためには、高等裁判所がこの結論に至った推論を整理する必要がある。
裁判所は事実認定において以下のように判示した。
当該宗教法人は1964年に登録され、本部組織と地方組織から構成されていた。2022年12月時点で、日本全国に67の教区と285の教会が存在した。
収入と予算
教会の収入の97%以上は信者からの献金で構成されていた。
教会の年度予算は以下に基づいて決定されていた。
各部門(本部の各局、各地区本部、各教区本部、各教会)が前年度の実績に基づき作成した予算原案
本部の各局の事業計画および海外宣教支援計画に基づく本部支出計画案
本部予算局が、各教会から本部に送金される献金見込額および支出計画案を調整した上で、教会全体の予算原案を作成し、理事会がこれを承認するという手順
したがって予算には、翌年度の必要経費を賄うために集めるべき献金の数値目標が含まれていた。
職員によるこのような予算策定や、全国的に見込まれる献金額そのものに、特異な点や常軌を逸した点はない。
それにもかかわらず裁判所は、これらの数字が社会的に許容される水準と比較して極めて高額であると判断した。一方で同じ判決の中で、これらの献金が2022年における285教会の運営、2024年時点での1,933人の職員の雇用、および2,441人の扶養家族の生活を支えていたことも認定している。
裁判所は、具体的な数値目標が設定されていたことのみを根拠として、これらの献金が「社会的に許容される」範囲を逸脱した手段によらなければ得られなかったし、今後も得られないと結論付けた。
宗教教義および教えに対する評価
裁判所はその後、教会の宗教教義および教え、ならびに創始者である文鮮明師の教えについて、6ページにわたる評価を行った。
裁判所は宗教的信念および教義を検討し、文師および教会指導者が日本人信者から組織的に献金を勧誘し、最後の一銭まで引き出そうとしたと示そうとした。同時に裁判所は、これらの資金が宣教活動や人道的事業の財源に充てられていたことを認めており、これらはあらゆる宗教において一般的かつ保護された慣行である。
国家の中立義務に照らして容認しがたいこの偏った評価は、文脈から切り離された引用とされるものに基づいており、裁判所はそこから、教会の唯一の目的は信者に個人的犠牲を払ってでも献金を行うよう促し、資金を集めることにあると結論付けた。
「霊感商法」をめぐる過去の主張
裁判所は、教会活動の枠外で信者によって行われた「霊感商法」を検討した。
ただし裁判所は、これらの活動を教会による法令違反の根拠とはしなかった。これらの活動には教会の代表者や職員が関与していなかったためである。裁判所がこれらに言及したのは、もっぱら本件を不利な印象で描くためであったと思われる。
裁判所は、1970年代に統一教会員によって設立された民間企業ハッピーワールドを検討した。同社の主たる事業は、韓国産高麗人参茶の訪問販売、および韓国産大理石製骨壺の輸入販売であった。
販売員2名が顧客に契約を締結させるため不当な圧力をかけたとして起訴され、1984年に執行猶予付きの懲役刑を言い渡された。同社は1987年に営業を停止した。
裁判所はまた、2007年から2009年にかけて特定商取引法違反、すなわち訪問販売をめぐって発出された3件の略式命令にも言及した。これらは罰金刑を科すものであった。
2009年11月10日、裁判所は有限会社 新世に対し罰金刑を、信者3名に対し執行猶予付きの懲役刑および罰金刑を言い渡した。
これらの事実には教会の指導者や職員は関与しておらず、裁判所も「法令違反」の有無を判断する上でこれらを考慮の対象としなかった。
これらの事実は数十年前に存続を終えた営利企業に関するものであり、2026年に下された非営利の宗教法人の解散と結び付けることはできない。
26件の民事判決
公共の福祉に対する損害を立証するため、裁判所が代わりに依拠したのは、1979年から2014年の間に発生した事象に関する26件の確定民事判決であった。これらの大半は、強制的な「ディプログラミング」(脱会説得)を受けさせられた元信者によって提起されたものであった——以下「最終判決」という。
最終判決において、各民事裁判所は、教会員による「社会的相当性」を逸脱した「不適切な献金勧誘」について、教会の民事責任を認めた。
この行為は「不適切な献金勧誘行為」と性格付けられ、以下の主張から構成されていた。
一般市民が参加する入門セミナーにおいて教会の身元を秘匿したこと
「因縁」に言及して不安を煽り、一般市民から宗教的献金を獲得したこと
それによって献金者の自由意思および選択の権限を侵害したこと
申立人らの主張の大部分が信用性を欠くものであり、強制下でなされたものであるという問題に加え、これら26件の民事判決で争われた事実は1979年から2014年の期間に遡るものであり、別の必要性が認められない限り、現時点で解散という極端な措置を正当化することはできない。
特にこれらの事実は、2009年に教会が発出した「コンプライアンス宣言」以前のものである。同宣言において教会は、適用される法令を遵守し、こうした主張を未然に防ぐための具体的な指示を出していた。

本記事はBitter Winterに掲載された記事の翻訳である。
原典のクレジットはBitter Winterに帰属する。
https://bitterwinter.org/unification-church-japans-dissolution-order-and-international-law-5-was-the-order-needed/

title: What is Chongryon? North Korea's Outpost in Japan — and a Problem Left Unresolved for 80 Years
previewText: The debate over Japan's proposed anti-espionage law and foreign agents registration system under the Takaichi administration is bringing renewed attention to the General Association of Korean

blocks.text: The debate over Japan's proposed anti-espionage law and foreign agents registration system under the Takaichi administration is bringing renewed attention to the General Association of Korean Residents in Japan (Chongryon), an organization that has occupied a gray zone in postwar Japan for seven decades.
It is distinct from Mindan, the separate organization representing pro-South Korea ethnic Koreans in Japan.

Chongryon is not simply an ethnic community group. It represents only the pro-North Korea segment of Koreans in Japan. It is North Korea's effective overseas outpost, operating inside Japanese territory. Tied directly to the Workers' Party of Korea, it has built a network spanning education, finance, media, and culture. And most of what it actually does remains poorly understood.
1. What kind of organization
Chongryon was founded in 1955 and openly supports North Korea.

Japan and North Korea have no diplomatic ties. Chongryon issues travel documents on behalf of the North Korean government for those Koreans in Japan affiliated with the pro-North Korea community. It works, in effect, as North Korea's embassy.
Active membership is estimated at around 70,000, out of roughly 450,000 ethnic Koreans living in Japan today.
The Public Security Intelligence Agency lists Chongryon as a target of investigation under the Subversive Activities Prevention Act.

The Japanese government's view is clear. In March 2012, then-National Public Safety Commission Chairman Nakai Hiroshi told the House of Representatives Budget Committee that Chongryon "is a group of ethnic Koreans in Japan who support North Korea. The group has been linked to serious international terrorism and abduction cases, and is seen as having an extremely close relationship with North Korea."
2. How far it has spread
Chongryon's reach extends well beyond a typical ethnic association.

Education — Chosen schools (Chongryon-affiliated, North Korea–aligned ethnic schools) totaled 55 nationwide as of May 2024. Enrollment fell from a peak of 40,000 to 6,000 by 2016. Principal appointments and curriculum are effectively decided by Chongryon headquarters and the Workers' Party of Korea.
Finance — At its peak, 16 Chogin credit unions across Japan (addressed below)
Entertainment — Wide involvement in the pachinko industry
Media and propaganda — The Chōsen Shinpo newspaper and numerous affiliated entities

Mass mobilization — In 2010, an internal document showed Chongryon had instructed parents to pose as Japanese nationals and flood the education ministry with phone calls demanding tuition waivers for Chosen schools
The structure pulls education, finance, entertainment, media, and mass mobilization under a single command.
3. History: Peak to decline
Cold War peak (1955–1980s).

Chongryon grew rapidly as North Korea's largest overseas outpost. Educational aid and scholarships from North Korea to Chosen schools, channeled through Chongryon, totaled about 46 billion yen by 2010.
The 1959–1984 repatriation program sent more than 93,000 people to North Korea.
Post-Cold War retreat (1990s–2001)

With the collapse of the Cold War order and North Korea's worsening economy, the organization's credibility began to crack. From 1997, the Chogin credit unions tied to Chongryon started failing one after another. Suspicions over the group's role in the abductions of Japanese citizens also began surfacing, and public opinion shifted.
Decline after 2002

The decisive blow came at the September 2002 Japan–North Korea summit. When Kim Jong Il publicly admitted the abductions, Chongryon lost what remained of its legitimacy. Members left in growing numbers, and in 2014 its headquarters building, already under court seizure, was sold at auction.
Even so, the organization has not disappeared. In April 2025, Kim Jong Un sent Chongryon more than $2 million (about 287 million yen) in educational support funds.
4. What Chosen schools teach: Juche ideology and anti-American content
Textbook content from these Chongryon-operated schools' Modern Korean History and Society, as recorded by Japan's education ministry, follows the official North Korean line.

"Our Republic is a socialist state guided by Juche ideology. Juche ideology, founded by the beloved great leader Comrade Kim Il Sung, is a human-centered worldview and a revolutionary ideology for realizing the independence of the popular masses." (Society, Senior Grade 3, emphasis added)

On the Korean War: "At the instigation of American imperialism, Syngman Rhee concentrated artillery fire on the Republic's territory along the 38th parallel starting June 23, 1950, and on June 25 expanded this into full-scale war."
According to the Public Security Intelligence Agency, third-year high school students take part in "homeland visits" to North Korea.
5. The agent network: Chōsen University's other face
Chōsen University (Chongryon-affiliated) in Kodaira, Tokyo, also functions as a recruitment pipeline for North Korean intelligence.

A 2012 Osaka Prefectural Police case shows the pattern. A man who graduated from a Chosen school and Chōsen University, worked as English editor of Chongryon's Korea Shinpo, and had joined the "Study Group," Chongryon's non-public organization, was found to have received payments from North Korean authorities as a "base person" sleeper agent.

He collected information on Japan Self-Defense Force weapons, equipment, and military communications technology. He infiltrated civil society groups working on North Korean human rights and kept watch on citizens who opposed tuition waivers for Chosen schools.
Japan has no anti-espionage law. Being a foreign spy is not in itself a crime.That legal gap has provided the operating environment for Chongryon's network.
6. The assassination of South Korea's First Lady — Chongryon, the Mangyongbong, and a stolen revolver
The clearest illustration of Chongryon's network role is the August 15, 1974 assassination attempt on South Korean President Park Chung-hee at the National Theater in Seoul.
The gunman was Mun Se-gwang, a 22-year-old ethnic Korean born in Osaka. A Chongryon Osaka branch political officer urged him to act and gave him 500,000 yen in operational funds.

He received orders from the North Korean government to assassinate Park, was trained, and met directly with North Korean agents aboard the Mangyongbong, anchored in Osaka Bay. He stole a revolver from a Japanese police box and entered Seoul on a forged passport.

At the Liberation Day ceremony, a bullet from Mun's revolver struck First Lady Yuk Young-soo in the head. A stray round from a security officer returning fire killed Jang Bong-hwa, a 17-year-old member of the choir.
According to South Korean prosecutors, just before his execution Mun reportedly told them:
"I was a fool to be deceived by Chongryon.”
A Chongryon official's incitement. Direct contact with Workers' Party agents on the Mangyongbong. A revolver stolen from a Japanese police box.
The case stands as decisive evidence that Chongryon is the core of an operational network embedded inside Japan.
7. Involvement in abductions of Japanese citizens
The Japanese government has officially acknowledged that Chongryon-affiliated individuals were involved in the 1978 abduction of Tanaka Minoru and the 1980 Shin Gwang Su case involving Hara Tadaaki.

The government has also confirmed Chongryon-affiliated involvement in a 1989 case in which a senior official of the Korean Chamber of Commerce in Japan attempted to illegally export goods restricted under COCOM to North Korea, and in a 1994 case involving the illegal export of jet mills usable in missile development.
8. "Paradise on earth": The largest mass deception in postwar Japan
The event that ruined the most lives in Chongryon's history was one of postwar Japan's largest mass deceptions — the "repatriation program."

What was the repatriation program?
From 1959 to 1984, ethnic Koreans living in Japan were sent to North Korea on a massive scale. Inside Japan, Chongryon ran the program.

Why did it happen?
The newly founded North Korea faced a serious labor shortage and needed to show the world that the Kim Il Sung regime was legitimate. Bringing ethnic Koreans "home" solved both problems at once. Chongryon called North Korea a "paradise on earth," promising free food, clothing, housing, education, and healthcare for all.

How many went?
In the end, 93,340 people moved to North Korea. Most of those sent to North Korea originated from what is now South Korea.
They were sent "home" to a country that was never their home, under Chongryon's promises of a "paradise on earth."
At least 6,839 of them were Japanese citizens — Japanese wives and husbands and their relatives.

What was the reality?
Nothing like the promises. Political purges. Prison camps. No freedom of movement. Censored mail. Only about 150 ever made it back to Japan.
Japan was also part of it.
At the time, about 600,000 ethnic Koreans were living in Japan.

Many were poor, and the Japanese government saw them as a burden on welfare and public order. Inoue Masutarō, an official sent from the Foreign Ministry to the Japanese Red Cross, wrote in a 1956 internal document: "To put it bluntly, the Japanese government has an interest in sweeping the troublesome Koreans out of Japan."
In February 1959, the Kishi Nobusuke cabinet approved the program. Major newspapers — Asahi, Sankei, Yomiuri — joined the campaign and ran supportive coverage.
2026: Tokyo court orders North Korea to pay damages.

On January 26, 2026, the Tokyo District Court ordered North Korea to pay about 88 million yen in damages to four plaintiffs who had been deceived by the false promises. Half a century later, justice finally caught up with one of postwar Japan's biggest deceptions.
9. Two Overlooked Connections
9-1. The Chogin collapse and 1.4 trillion yen of Japanese tax money

Between 1997 and 2002, 16 Chogin credit unions tied to Chongryon failed one after another. Total public funds used to resolve the failures reached about 1.36 trillion yen (some estimates put the figure at 1.4 trillion), roughly three times North Korea's annual state budget.
Prime Minister Abe Shinzo addressed the Diet on the matter:

"The collapse of the Chogin credit unions is different from other credit union failures. They knew insolvency was coming, and went on lending with the expectation that deposit insurance or public funds would eventually fill the hole. It was effectively illegal lending. Lenders and borrowers worked together, on the premise that money would flow to North Korea."
A sitting prime minister told the Diet that Japanese taxpayer money had flowed to North Korea by design.
By 2004, more than 25 Chogin executives, including the former finance director of Chongryon's central headquarters, had been arrested, and more than 150 questioned.
9-2. A former Public Security Intelligence Agency director helped the organization he had been tasked to monitor

In June 2007, it came to light that ownership of the Chongryon headquarters building in Tokyo had been transferred to a company run by Shigetake Ogata, the former head of Japan's Public Security Intelligence Agency.
The Public Security Intelligence Agency is the government body that monitors Chongryon under the Subversive Activities Prevention Act.
In other words, the man who had once led the agency watching Chongryon was now helping that same group avoid having its assets seized.

Ogata and others were charged with fraud, and the Supreme Court finalized the guilty verdict in May 2014. Even so, Ogata insisted he was innocent throughout the trial. He said the confession he gave while in detention had been forced out of him. What really happened in the case is still debated today.
10. The anti-espionage law and Chongryon
The Takaichi administration's proposed anti-espionage law and foreign agents registration system cut directly across the Chongryon issue.

On March 13, 2026, the cabinet approved the "National Intelligence Council Establishment Bill," and the law's core provision under consideration is a mandatory registration system requiring foreign agents operating in Japan to disclose their activities.

Chongryon itself has not issued a public statement against the legislation. The actual opponents are the Japan Communist Party, the Social Democratic Party, the Constitutional Democratic Party, labor unions, and bar associations — all of whom have had long-standing ties to Chongryon.
When the repatriation program was being launched, both the Socialist Party and the Communist Party led the support, with the Liberal Democratic Party and other parties also backing it.

Given that Chongryon is widely regarded as North Korea's effective embassy in Japan, it would clearly fall within the scope of organizations subject to registration and disclosure once the foreign agents system takes effect.
Conclusion
Chongryon is the symbol of a problem postwar Japan has avoided facing directly.

The gun that struck down a former South Korean First Lady in Seoul. The repatriation program that deceived 93,000 people. Involvement in multiple abduction cases. Agents who stole Self-Defense Forces information from Chōsen University in Tokyo. 1.4 trillion yen in tax money. The collusion involving a former Public Security Intelligence Agency chief.
These are not separate incidents. They are the history of one organization.

Each time something happened, Japan handled it as its own problem. Repatriation was a humanitarian issue. Abductions were a diplomatic issue. Spies were security cases. Tax money was a local government debate.
The line that connects all of them has almost never been discussed.
Eighty years after the war. Japan still has no answer to what Chongryon actually is.
